name: Compile a metrics report with PostgreSQL and Microsoft Teams for your team

parameters.query: SELECT * FROM emails WHERE updated_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 day' ORDER BY updated_at DESC;

INSERT INTO email_log (messageId, from, subject, logged_at)
VALUES ($1, $2, $3, NOW());

parameters.content:
### Daily Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — daily at 9:00. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Read Recent from PostgreSQL

### Read Recent from PostgreSQL

postgres — `executeQuery` operation against the connected account.

Next: Newest First

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Postgres connection

### Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Trim to Digest Size

### Trim to Digest Size

Caps the stream at 15 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Summarize Content (AI)

### Summarize Content (AI)

Map-reduce summarization chain — handles long content without blowing the context window.

Next: Post Digest to Microsoft Teams

### Groq Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Summarize Content (AI)** as `languageModel`

🔑 Groq API key

### Post Digest to Microsoft Teams

microsoftTeams — `chatMessage` operation against the connected account.

Next: Archive Digest in PostgreSQL

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Post Digest to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
### Archive Digest in PostgreSQL

postgres — `executeQuery` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Postgres connection

# Compile a metrics report with PostgreSQL and Microsoft Teams for your team

**Pattern:** Scheduled Digest · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Compile a metrics report with PostgreSQL and Microsoft Teams for your team. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual drip campaigns is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Daily Schedule → Read Recent from PostgreSQL → Newest First → Trim to Digest Size → Summarize Content (AI) → Post Digest to Microsoft Teams → Archive Digest in PostgreSQL

## Use it for
- Teams that want to scheduled digest without manual effort
- Anyone automating drip campaigns in marketing
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Standardizes the process every time
- Keeps your systems in sync

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Groq API key; Microsoft OAuth2; Postgres connection.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your drip campaigns process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
